title: Amauropelta inaequilateralis
taxonomy: Amauropelta inaequilateralis is a species of fern in the family Thelychriedae. It belongs to the genus Amauropelta, which is a genus of ferns. The species Amauropelta inaequilateralis is native to South America and is part of the larger family of ferns known as Polypodiophyta. The classification of Amauropelta inaequilateralis is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Division: Pteridophyta, Class: Polypodiopsida, Order: Polypodiales, Family: Thelychriedae, Genus: Amauropelta, Species: A. inaequilateralis. This species of fern is found in subtropical regions of South America.

intro: Amauropelta inaequilateralis, also known as the Inequilateral Raspfern or Inequilateral Lady Fern, is a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which can aid in the treatment of various health conditions, such as fever, digestive issues, and skin infections.
